soap:
RE-REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#2915 — chore: migrate Gemini embeddings to use `google-genai` SDK
reviewed 46337281 → b5971ddb  (1 commit, 1 file)
domain: python/lancedb/embeddings

SUBJECTIVE
I approved this PR cleanly at 46337281 on 2026-01-21 with no inline comments
and no blocking conditions. The author pushed one commit since. The only
question on the thread is markmcd asking whether some failing CI tests relate
to this change (2026-01-27) — a CI question, not a code-review condition.

OBJECTIVE
Delta 46337281...b5971ddb, scoped to the PR's own files (0 off-branch files
excluded), is a single one-line change in
python/python/lancedb/embeddings/gemini_text.py:

    @@ -140,5 +140,5 @@ def client(self):
                     "x-goog-api-client": f"lancedb/{__version__}",
                 }
    -            }
    +            },
             )

A trailing comma added after the `http_options={...}` argument in the
`genai.Client(...)` call (line 143 at head). Valid Python, almost certainly a
formatter (ruff/black) normalization. No semantic change. The embeddings
migration code I approved last cycle is otherwise untouched.

ASSESSMENT
CONDITIONS LEDGER (0/0 met)
  No prior blocking conditions to verify — my last review was a clean approval
  with zero issue(blocking) comments. Nothing to reconstruct or re-confirm.

FRESH-EYES DELTA
  No new concerns. The sole change since my last review is a cosmetic trailing
  comma inside a call-argument list; it cannot alter runtime behavior and
  cannot cause the test failures markmcd referenced (a comma in a kwargs dict
  literal is a no-op semantically). Nothing new and bad was introduced.

  Note (non-blocking, informational only — CI is out of scope for a
  diff-anchored re-review): markmcd's question about failing tests is unrelated
  to anything in this delta. If the attending wants to close that loop it would
  be by checking the CI run, not the code.

PLAN
RECOMMENDATION: approve (re-affirm prior approval)
  confidence: high · the entire delta since my approval is one trailing comma
  with no behavioral effect.
  what would make this wrong: if the formatter change were a smokescreen
  masking a force-push that rewrote earlier commits — but the compare is
  anchored to my exact reviewed sha (46337281...b5971ddb) and shows only this
  one hunk, so there is no hidden change.
